items.title: Clarification on gold holdings
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has come across reports in certain sections of the media about RBI’s sale of gold. The RBI emphasizes that these reports are not correct. In this context, it is clarified that the physical stock of gold is disclosed by RBI in its Monthly Bulletin. The latest edition is available on the RBI website at: https://www.rbi.org.in/Scripts/BS_ViewBulletin.aspx?Id=24213 , and the physical stock of gold remains unchanged at 880.52 tonnes as on date. Members of public are, therefore, advised to rely on official information published by RBI from time to time in such matters

items.title: IIFL Finance set to close debut social bond after $1.7 bn demand
items.summary: The non-banking financial company tightened pricing to 7.6% from an initial guidance of 7.9%, after investor demand exceeded $1.7 billion.

items.title: KNAV secures follow-on investment from Nikhil Kamath’s NKSquared
items.summary: Global accounting firm KNAV has secured follow-on investment from NKSquared, the investment firm of Nikhil Kamath. This funding will fuel international acquisitions and investments in people and capabilities. KNAV has seen significant revenue growth and international expansion since its initial funding in September 2024.

items.title: Google upsizes historic equity raise to $85bn to back AI spending spree
items.summary: Tech giant’s first stock offering in more than two decades gets strong demand as investors shrug off huge investment plans

items.title: IRCTC sets new records in online ticket booking, blocks 3 crore suspicious IDs in 2025-26
items.summary: IRCTC intensified anti-fraud measures in 2025, deactivating 3.03 crore suspicious user IDs and blocking 13,343 email domains. The corporation also recorded record online ticket bookings, averaging 14.53 lakh per day, while strengthening cybersecurity to ensure fair access for genuine passengers.

items.title: GQG Partners sells 1.8% stake in GMR Airports for Rs 1,906 crore; Fidelity buys holding
items.summary: US investment firm GQG Partners has sold a 1.8 per cent stake in GMR Airports to Fidelity International. The deal, valued at Rs 1,906 crore, saw GQG Partners offload 19.50 crore shares. Fidelity International acquired the same number of shares. This transaction occurred as GMR Airports reported a profit of Rs 400.49 crore for the March quarter.

items.title: Suzlon to diversify beyond wind; pump Rs 500 cr in new arm this fiscal
items.summary: Wind turbine maker Suzlon is diversifying into battery storage and a new platform called RE DevCo. This move aims to make Suzlon a complete renewable energy solutions provider. The company plans significant investments in the coming years. Suzlon also has ambitious goals to expand its renewable energy sales and orderbook substantially by 2031.

items.title: Led by Poland & China, central banks turned net buyers of gold in April
items.summary: The development comes after they were net sellers of the precious metal in March; Russia continues to offload the yellow metal

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield nears 4.5% again after strong jobs data, rise in oil
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields increased Wednesday as traders await new economic data amid ongoing Middle East tensions.

items.title: Sensex, Nifty trim losses as banking stocks offset IT rout, focus shifts to RBI MPC meeting
items.summary: BSE Sensex ends 303.67 points lower at 73,492.60; NSE Nifty 50 settles 78 points lower at 23,405.60

items.title: Tata Motors rethinks its premium EV playbook
items.summary: Tata Motors has revamped its premium EV brand, Avinya, by shifting from its original platform plans to leveraging partnerships and ready-made architectures. This strategic pivot aims to accelerate the launch of its luxury EVs, with the first model slated for a 2027 India rollout, utilizing the Freelander platform from Chery Jaguar Land Rover.

items.title: Gulf states in talks for oil pipelines to bypass Hormuz
items.summary: Oil exporters seek alternative ways to export, as crucial waterway has been disrupted for over three months

items.title: SpaceX targets fixed $135 IPO roadshow price at $1.75 trillion valuation, source says
items.summary: The valuation would make Elon Musk's company the seventh-biggest in the U.S., above Tesla, which has a market cap of about $1.6 trillion.

items.title: Govt considering policy to support affordable adoption of E85 fuel: Hardeep Singh Puri
items.summary: The Indian government is actively considering a policy to accelerate the affordable adoption of E85 fuel, a blend of 85% ethanol with petrol. This move follows the unveiling of India's first flex-fuel motorcycles compatible with E85, signaling a significant step in the nation's energy transition.

items.title: Dulux owner Akzo Nobel closes 17% lower after takeover talks collapse; European stocks fall
items.summary: Investors are also monitoring developments in the U.S.-Iran war after tensions escalated overnight.

items.title: Bata India bets on improving inventory reduction through efficiency steps, omni-channel strategy
items.summary: Bata India emphasizes inventory reduction through efficiency, with a 13% decrease in gross inventory in the latest quarter. The company's omni-channel strategy and product expansion plans signal future growth despite challenges.

items.title: Fuel price shield helping India and consumers to tackle West Asia crisis: Gita Gopinath
items.summary: India is already cushioning the economy from the West Asia crisis. Gita Gopinath, former IMF Deputy Managing Director, states the government is limiting fuel price hikes. This acts as an implicit subsidy. She advises more targeted support for vulnerable households and businesses instead of broad fuel subsidies. This approach helps where needed most and avoids economic distortions.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Ather Energy among 2 stock recommendations for Thursday
items.summary: Domestic markets rebounded from early losses despite rising Iran-US tensions, supported by strong gains in banking stocks. Analysts have recommended Ather Energy and J&K Bank for Thursday, citing bullish breakouts, healthy volumes, strong momentum indicators and favorable long-term technical structures.

items.title: Former UBS private credit fund says law firm helped defraud it of $145mn
items.summary: Fund alleges Pillsbury Winthrop lawyer sent it ‘fake financials’ to secure loans linked to Aspiration Partners

items.title: BHEL shares to be in focus on Thursday as company bags ₹2,500 crore international order from Nigeria’s Dangote
items.summary: In an exchange filing on Wednesday, the company said it has signed a contract agreement with Dangote Petroleum Refinery & Petrochemicals Free Zone Enterprise.

items.title: Guwahati Tea Auction Centre sets new benchmark for quality tea prices
items.summary: Guwahati Tea Auction Centre achieved record prices for quality tea. Deckiajuli Tea Estate sold premium CTC leaf at Rs 1,103 per kg. Sotai Tea Estate tea also reached a new high of Rs 889 per kg. Over 65,000 kg of tea sold above Rs 400 per kg. The Assam Government offers subsidies for orthodox tea.

items.title: SoftBank sells Rs 2,873 crore worth of Lenskart shares in block deal to Goldman Sachs and other investors
items.summary: Lenskart Solutions saw a major stake sale as SoftBank offloaded shares worth about Rs 2,873 crore through a block deal. The transaction drew strong institutional interest, while analysts remain positive on Lenskart's growth prospects, technology-led model and expanding international footprint.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Indian equities remained volatile amid IT sector weakness, global uncertainties and cautious investor sentiment. Analysts see key Nifty support near 23,200-23,000 and resistance around 23,750-23,800. Market participants will monitor technical levels, global cues, sectoral trends and stock-specific developments for direction.

items.title: Partners Group limits withdrawals at private equity fund for wealthy individuals
items.summary: Swiss private capital firm caps redemptions from its $8.6bn flagship vehicle

items.title: India became world's second largest solar market in 2025: Bhupender Yadav
items.summary: India has become the world's second-largest solar market, surpassing the US in annual capacity additions for 2025. The nation's solar capacity has surged by over 5,370% since 2014, now exceeding 155 GW. This rapid clean energy growth has enabled India to meet its Paris Agreement climate targets ahead of schedule.

items.title: Why FirstCry excelled at selling diapers but hasn’t quite cracked the pre-school code
items.summary: Parents buy diapers online, but choose preschools offline. FirstCry thought its vast baby product experience and ecosystem would help it crack the adjacency of preschools. Six years on, Intellitots is profitable but not even halfway near its 1,000-school ambition.

items.title: HUL employees on permanent roll down 10.7% to 5,898 in FY26
items.summary: Hindustan Unilever Limited's permanent employee count will decrease by 10.7 percent to 5,898 by March 2026. The company is investing significantly in expanding manufacturing for premium products. Median employee remuneration is projected to rise by 6.08 percent. HUL is also focusing on workplace inclusivity, with women comprising 44 percent of its managerial staff.

items.title: West Asia war slows mutual fund growth in GIFT City
items.summary: For global investors looking to put money into India, the process is a breeze through GIFT City

items.title: Israeli startup Coralogix raises $200 mn, to allocate 20% to India biz
items.summary: Coralogix, which had entered India in 2020, had announced that it would invest $30 million over three years into the country.

items.title: Oil prices rise after Netanyahu tells CNBC Israel and U.S. prepared to attack Iran again if necessary
items.summary: U.S. Central Command said Tuesday that it had defeated multiple Iranian ballistic missiles following "attempted attacks" by Iran.

items.title: Tweaks to the BoJ’s balance sheet plans in June could follow bond volatility
items.summary: Bank of Japan has been slowly reducing its bond purchases to speed up quantitative tightening efforts

items.title: Elevated oil prices could drag India's growth to 6%, below IMF forecast of 6.5%: Gita Gopinath
items.summary: Elevated oil prices could slow India's economic growth to around 6%, according to former IMF official Gita Gopinath. She warned that prolonged West Asian tensions could further impact global growth and India's prospects. Gopinath emphasized supply-side reforms, renewable energy, and improving ease of doing business to boost investment and strengthen India's global supply chain role.

items.title: Sebi flags ₹15 trillion misstatement in Rajesh Exports accounts
items.summary: Sebi has alleged that ₹15.15 trillion in subsidiary revenue was misrepresented, and flagged gaps in disclosures and inconsistencies in filings. It has also barred promoter Rajesh Mehta from trading in the shares of Rajesh Exports until further orders.

items.title: KKR, Ares, Blackstone tumble as Partners Group caps private equity fund withdrawals
items.summary: U.S. private markets firms fell on Wednesday, as concerns over redemption pressure returned to the spotlight.

items.title: US announces new tariffs over forced labour concerns
items.summary: It comes after the US Supreme Court struck down many of US President Donald Trump's previous duties in February.

items.title: SEBI bars Rajesh Mehta from trading in Rajesh Exports shares
items.summary: Regulator alleges misrepresentation of over ₹15 lakh crore in revenues linked to overseas subsidiaries; orders fresh forensic audit

items.title: Bengaluru based prop-tech startup Propsoch secures $2 million in seed funding
items.summary: The funding will strengthen its core research and advisory capabilities, expand its internal teams across various business functions, and support its strategic expansion into new regional markets

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold tumbles $53/oz; silver drops $2 amid firm dollar, inflation fears
items.summary: Comex gold futures slipped back into losses, falling $53 per troy ounce to the day’s low of $4,467, while silver futures also declined nearly $2 to trade around $74 per ounce.

items.title: MoSPI releases uniform norms for DDP estimates with 2022-23 base year​
items.summary: The Statistics Ministry has released uniform guidelines for compiling District Domestic Product (DDP) estimates with a 2022-23 base year. These guidelines, finalized after stakeholder consultation, aim to ensure consistency and comparability in district-level economic data. The framework elaborates on concepts, data sources, and estimation procedures, emphasizing a bottom-up approach where possible.

items.title: Propsoch raises $2 million in funding round led by Athera Venture Partners
items.summary: Bengaluru-based Propsoch secured $2 million in a funding round. Athera Venture Partners, Sparrow Capital, and Vakil Group led the investment. The funds will enhance research, expand the team, and support market growth. Propsoch aims to reduce homebuyer regret by offering unbiased recommendations. The company plans to expand its services to Mumbai.

items.title: Aurobindo Pharma enters biologics contract manufacturing with Rs 1,200-crore Telangana facility
items.summary: Aurobindo Pharma has launched TheraNym, a major biologics manufacturing plant in Telangana. This facility will produce biologic medicines for global markets. US pharma giant MSD is the first major client. The plant signifies Aurobindo's expansion into the growing biologics and contract manufacturing sector. It is set to create over 1,500 jobs.

items.title: Fed Chair Warsh makes first hires at central bank, including 'Project 2025' author
items.summary: The new Fed chair has made his first hires. One wrote the Fed chapter in the conservative policy blueprint "Project 2025."

items.title: RBI’s Swaminathan warns banks on rising risks beyond balance sheets
items.summary: Indian banks face a new era of resilience. RBI Deputy Governor Swaminathan J highlighted the need to manage complex uncertainties from geopolitics, climate change, and AI. This shift moves beyond balance sheet stress. Banks must adapt to unpredictable risks. The Indian financial system is entering this phase with strong fundamentals.

items.title: ADB, StanChart ink partnership to support Indian firms across supply chains
items.summary: The Asian Development Bank and Standard Chartered Bank have partnered to boost supply chain finance in India. Agreements include risk-sharing for USD transactions via GIFT City and a guarantee facility for rupee transactions, aiming to expand access for businesses and enhance trade flows.

items.title: Rajesh Exports: Sebi finds 97-99% revenue inflation, bars promoter from trading
items.summary: Rajesh Exports and promoter Rajesh Mehta face regulatory action after Sebi alleged large-scale financial misrepresentation and possible revenue inflation of 97-99%. The regulator has barred Mehta from trading in company securities and ordered a fresh forensic audit.

items.title: Trump says Iran agreed to not have nuclear weapons, but 'they can change their mind'
items.summary: The president was speaking to the New York Post's "Pod Force One" podcast in a wide-ranging interview.

items.title: Lloyds, Halifax and Bank of Scotland back after online banking outage
items.summary: "We're aware some customers are having issues with our app and online banking. We're really sorry about this," Lloyds Bank posted on X.

items.summary: The results were supported by lower expenses and a 65% rise in its collectibles business, which continues to evolve into a surprisingly important business line for the retailer.

items.summary: US stocks opened lower as rising crude oil prices and renewed Middle East tensions pressured sentiment, after reports of Iranian missile activity near Kuwait and Bahrain. Dow, S&P 500 and Nasdaq slipped at open, while concerns over new US tariff proposals added to risk-off mood despite strong AI-led earnings support.

items.title: CMR Green Technologies IPO: Issue received solid response on Day 01, booked 2.45 times; GMP signals bumper listing
items.summary: As of today, the grey market premium (GMP) for the CMR Green Technologies stands at ₹67 per share, suggesting that the stock is likely to list above its issue price. Based on this GMP and the upper price band, the estimated listing price for the stock stood at ₹259, reflecting a 35% premium.

items.title: Universal park officially named as government pledges £1.3bn
items.summary: The government's contribution is going towards upgrading local infrastructure and transport links.

items.title: Alphabet's $80 billion stock sale leaves Wall Street in 'unprecedented territory,' says Goldman's Gutman
items.summary: Goldman Sachs is acting as a joint book-running manager for Alphabet's underwritten offerings.

items.title: Coal block 'scam': Delhi court discharges Hindalco
items.summary: A Delhi court has discharged Hindalco Industries and two former executives in a coal block case. The court found no evidence of illegal acts or criminal conspiracy. The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) failed to prove criminal breach of trust or cheating. The court stated that the allocation letter did not confer vested rights.

items.title: Cabinet clears ₹20,000 cr schemes for jet fuel support, cleaner Delhi vehicles
items.summary: One-time budgetary support of up to ₹10,000 crore for OMCs to provide ATF price stabilization amid high crude prices. The budgetary support will be in the form of interest-free advances to OMCs.

items.title: Kotak Alts secures final close of $1 billion for 14th real estate fund
items.summary: ADIA’s anchor commitment of over $675 million marks its sixth consecutive commitment to Kotak Alts’ real estate platform

items.title: Cabinet approves widening of NH-63 Armoor-Mancherial section under Hybrid Annuity Model
items.summary: The government has approved significant road development projects in Telangana. The Armoor-Jagtial-Mancherial section of NH-63 will be widened. The Jagtial-Karimnagar section of NH-563 will be four-laned. These projects aim to ease congestion and improve connectivity. Travel times are expected to reduce significantly. The initiatives will boost regional development and logistics.

items.title: RBI likely to hold rates as West Asia crisis impact on growth remains unclear: Bank of Baroda Report
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is expected to hold interest rates steady on Friday, according to Bank of Baroda. The central bank will likely maintain a data-dependent approach, balancing growth concerns with rising inflation risks. An upward revision in inflation forecasts is anticipated due to increased fuel prices and ongoing global uncertainties.

items.summary: SBI Securities has recommended subscribing to the issue, citing strong growth prospects in the recycled aluminium market and the company’s leadership position in the sector

items.title: Kotak Alts raises ADIA-anchored $1 bn real estate fund
items.summary: Kotak Alts has closed its 14th real estate fund at $1 billion with major investments from Abu Dhabi's ADIA and NPS Korea. This marks the latter's first commitment in Indian alternative assets.

items.title: JioHotstar ropes in 50 brands for Dhurandhar The Revenge digital premiere
items.summary: JioHotstar is launching Dhurandhar The Revenge on June 4. Fifty brands, including eleven major sponsors, have partnered for the digital premiere. This reflects increasing advertiser interest in major OTT film releases. The film will stream in multiple languages. JioHotstar anticipates a large audience for this high-engagement entertainment property.

items.title: Silver falls 1% to ₹2.64 lakh/kg amid weak global trends

items.title: Quote of the day by Tom Gayner: "Equities are congealed intellectual capital, and that is what I want"
items.summary: Investor Tom Gayner views stocks as ownership in businesses built on human ingenuity. He emphasizes that a company's value stems from its ideas, expertise, and innovation. Successful firms convert knowledge into profits, creating lasting economic value. These businesses continuously reinvest in their intellectual capital, attracting talent and fostering growth.

items.title: India insurance market to grow 10.7% annually over next decade, fastest among major markets, says Allianz
items.summary: India's insurance market is set for rapid expansion, becoming the world's fastest-growing. Projections show significant annual growth over the next decade. This surge is fueled by a large underinsured population and evolving demographics. Key segments like health, life, and property and casualty insurance will see substantial increases. Government initiatives aim to boost access and stability.

items.summary: US stock futures are stable amid Middle East tensions and rising crude oil prices. Uncertainty surrounds US-Iran peace talks as Washington retaliates against Tehran with strikes on Qeshm Island.

items.title: Borosil Renewables jumps after import duties’ extension
items.summary: The stock jumped as much as 9.3% in Mumbai on Wednesday, the most in more than a year, after India’s finance ministry announced a five-year extension of the so-called countervailing duties

items.title: India-US trade agreement "99% there", last 1% being finalized: US Ambassador Sergio Gor
items.summary: India and the United States are nearing a significant trade agreement. US Ambassador Sergio Gor revealed negotiations are almost complete. Both nations are finalizing the last technical details. This deal is expected to be a win-win for both countries. Ambassador Gor is meeting with Union Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal to discuss the pact.

items.title: How have crude oil prices behaved during the West Asia war?
items.summary: The West Asia war pushed Brent crude from about $70 to a peak of $126 per barrel, raising fuel costs and inflation in India, which imports 90% of its oil. Prices have eased amid Iran-US peace talks but remain elevated, keeping pressure on growth, consumers and businesses.

items.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: IT, FMCG stocks weigh on markets; Sensex settles 303 pts lower, Nifty drops 78 pts
items.summary: Sensex, Nifty, Stock Price Highlights: Indian equities traded lower despite strong global cues, though markets recovered from intraday lows. Sensex ended 303.67 pts or 0.41% lower at 73,492.60 (recovering from the day’s low of 74,346.17). Nifty 50 settled 77.95 pts or 0.33% lower at 23,405.60.

items.summary: The project, spread across nearly 2 acres, has 5 lakh sq ft of built-up area. It will have 96 apartments.

items.title: Uttar Pradesh RERA cautions against panic buying of homes, says sufficient inventory available for sale
items.summary: Uttar Pradesh real estate regulator RERA reveals over 1.15 lakh housing and commercial units are available for sale across the state. Buyers have ample options for their needs and budgets. Developers and brokers claiming a supply crunch or imminent price rise are cautioned. Prospective buyers should verify project details on the RERA portal before making decisions. Property investment is a long-term financial commitment.

items.summary: A China-driven supply realignment, cutting gas imports by 45% and oil by 30%, has effectively added 7.5 million barrels daily to global markets, neutralising energy price fears. items.summary: Indian tyre exports reached a record Rs 27,312 crore in FY2025-26. The United States remained the top destination. items.summary: The Indian stock market fell on June 3, with the Nifty 50 down 0.36% and the Sensex down 0.54%. Rising crude oil prices and escalating Middle East tensions contributed to the decline, impacting investor confidence and market sentiment.

items.summary: India's refined fuel exports dropped significantly in May, reaching their lowest point since October 2022. items.summary: Novo is "all in" on launching the Wegovy pill in overseas markets, and it sees it as a "major opportunity."

items.summary: India's central bank is likely to maintain current interest rates. Despite rising fuel costs and potential monsoon issues, a key inflation measure remains low. items.summary: India is taking steps to manage its currency's decline. Higher duties on gold and silver imports are in place. Import rules for silver have been tightened.
